steps.title: Translate the notation
steps.content: $f(0)$ means the output value of the function when the input is $x=0$. On a graph of $y=f(x)$, that is the $y$-coordinate of the point on the curve directly above or below $x=0$.

steps.title: Read the graph at x = 0
steps.content: The line $x=0$ is the $y$-axis. The curve crosses the $y$-axis at the point $(0,3)$, so the output when $x=0$ is $3$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Therefore $f(0)=3$. The problem says the value is an integer, and the graph read gives 3.
